findingDetails.title: Completed research phases retain pre-run continuity state
findingDetails.evidence: Parent continuity remains 0% and directs phase 001 execution while the phase map marks 001-003 complete; phase-002/003 spec continuity remains 10% and directs already-completed research dispatch while their body status and implementation summaries report complete/100%.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact continuity/status inventory across the parent and all ten configured child packets found stale completed-phase continuity in the parent and phase-002/003 specs; planned phases 004-010 consistently remain 0% with unchecked checklists.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile the stale continuity blocks through the owning continuity workflow and refresh derived metadata.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: The planned manifest and hydration CLI has generation guards but no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion, so a conforming implementation may read outside styles/.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped exact search across phase 004/006/007/010 planning artifacts found no filesystem-containment control; phase 004 is the shared filesystem producer.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-004 contract permits hydration outside the styles root because no containment or symlink invariant is specified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all four assigned phase planning trees for realpath, canonicalization, traversal, escape, confinement, and symlink controls; reviewed generation and permitted-artifact language.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Manifest-derived fixed child paths or independent implementation containment may mitigate the risk, but neither is required or tested.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after canonical root containment, explicit symlink policy, and negative traversal fixtures become binding acceptance criteria.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: The planned transformer injects corpus-derived observations but specifies only an auditable envelope and literal/span leak gates, not instruction rejection or a data-only prompt boundary.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped search found envelope references across phase-006 artifacts but no behavioral prompt-injection criterion; phase-007 authority ordering does not sanitize prompt content.

claimAdjudication.claim: Corpus-borne instructions can influence generated prose while all planned phase-006 controls pass because the leak gate only tests copied values and spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched assigned phase trees for prompt injection, untrusted content, instruction rejection, sanitization, and boundary controls; reviewed de-literalization, locked FACTS, authority order, leak gates, and retry.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: A strict transformer could incidentally remove imperative text, but that behavior is neither specified nor tested.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after a closed data-only observation schema, instruction rejection, and behavioral injection fixtures become binding.

findingDetails.title: Canonical parent surfaces omit the implementation-phase dependency graph
findingDetails.evidence: The parent handoff table records only 001→002+ while child packets declare eleven implementation dependency edges and every planned child graph-metadata depends_on array is empty.
findingDetails.recommendation: Encode and validate the implementation DAG in the parent handoff map and canonical machine-readable dependency fields.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact dependency searches covered the parent map, all phase-004–010 planning artifacts, and every child graph metadata file; only child-local prose/tasks represent the edges.

claimAdjudication.claim: The canonical parent and graph metadata cannot represent or validate the implementation-phase ordering that child packets require.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed child dependency tables, blocked tasks, predecessor/successor references, parent transition rules, and all phase-004–010 graph dependency fields for an alternate canonical ordering representation.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child-local plans and blocked tasks encode the ordering and all implementation phases remain planned, limiting immediate execution risk; those controls do not populate the parent handoff contract or machine-readable graph.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if graph depends_on is proven not to model child-phase sequencing and the owning workflow proves child-local blockers are the sole authoritative, validated ordering source.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: The hydration contract has generation guards but no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-004 contract permits a conforming implementation to hydrate a corpus path that resolves outside the styles root because no containment or symlink invariant is specified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched phase-004/006/007/010 artifacts for realpath, canonicalization, traversal, root confinement, and symlink controls.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: The planned transformer injects corpus observations but specifies no data-only parsing, instruction rejection, or behavioral non-influence fixture.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Phase-006 artifacts repeat the envelope but lack prompt-injection behavior criteria; phase-007 authority ordering does not sanitize content.

claimAdjudication.claim: The planned phase-006 controls can pass while corpus-borne instructions influence generated prose, because the envelope is metadata and the leak gate only tests copied values/spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched planning artifacts for prompt injection, untrusted content, instruction rejection, sanitization, and injection-boundary controls.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: A strict transformer could incidentally discard imperatives, but current criteria do not require that property and leak signals do not detect non-literal instruction following.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the transformer becomes a closed data-only schema with instruction rejection and behavioral fixtures.

findingDetails.title: Canonical parent surfaces omit the implementation-phase dependency graph
findingDetails.evidence: Child packets declare eleven implementation dependency edges, but the parent handoff table and all planned child graph dependency arrays omit them.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Dependency searches covered the parent and every phase-004-010 child planning and graph surface.

claimAdjudication.claim: The canonical parent and graph metadata cannot represent or validate the implementation-phase ordering that child packets require.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed child dependency tables, blocked tasks, predecessor/successor references, parent transition rules, and all phase-004-010 graph fields.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child-local blockers encode ordering and limit immediate risk, but do not populate canonical parent or machine-readable graph surfaces.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if graph dependencies are proven unrelated to child sequencing and child blockers are the sole validated authority.

findingDetails.title: Two implementation packets classify existing mode and transport surfaces as new creations
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 009 marks existing foundations and motion contracts as Create in spec and plan; phase 010's spec marks the existing transport directory Create while its plan says Add.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact change-type searches covered phase-004-010 artifacts and direct reads proved all three runtime contracts already exist.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-009 and phase-010 owning specs classify three existing runtime surfaces as creations, permitting implementation to replace or independently scaffold contracts that must instead be extended.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed affected-surface plans, implementation wording, current contracts, and phase-008 modification conventions for evidence that Create meant additive files only.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Phase 009 later says additions and phase 010's plan says Add, reducing destructive intent; the owning action labels remain contradictory and phase 009 does not name the additive file boundary.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if dispatch ignores change labels and an authoritative affected-file manifest proves additive-only edits.

results.summary: Ownership is named, but three existing surfaces are classified as creations.
The shared seam owns common fields once and downstream phases reference or reuse it.
Contradictory create/add labels amplify reconciliation across foundations, motion, and transport.

findingDetails.title: Completed research phases retain pre-run continuity state
findingDetails.evidence: Parent and phase-002/003 specs retain obsolete actions/percentages while the phase map and implementation summaries report completion; normal resume precedence contains but does not erase canonical/no-redirect exposure.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Replayed parent pointer/list routing, child implementation-summary precedence, and canonical-doc fallback against all contradictory surfaces.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical packet surfaces give obsolete next actions and completion percentages for already-completed research phases.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Child implementation-summary precedence, parent pointer/list routing, null-pointer behavior, and --no-redirect exposure.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Normal child resume and parent child-list fallback contain immediate misrouting, but contradictory canonical metadata remains visible to fallback/no-redirect consumers.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Every completion, indexing, no-redirect, and canonical-doc consumer is proven to suppress the stale spec continuity fields.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: Hydration re-hashes manifest-selected artifacts but has no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion or negative fixture.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Owner requirements/tasks/checklist and STUDY consumer were searched for containment controls; only adjacent generation/rights controls exist.

claimAdjudication.claim: A conforming phase-004 implementation can hydrate a path resolving outside the styles root because containment and symlink invariants are unspecified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Realpath, canonical containment, traversal, symlink, root escape, and outside-root negative fixtures across producer and consumer.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Manifest paths and generation re-hashing may constrain reads in a future implementation, but neither is a binding root-containment invariant.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Canonical containment, explicit symlink policy, immutable safe paths, and negative traversal/escape fixtures become binding.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: Corpus observations enter the prompt, while specified leak tests cover copied literals/spans rather than instruction rejection or behavioral non-influence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Transformer, prompt builder, plan builder, runGuided retry, fixture matrix, and phase-007 authority order were replayed as one producer/consumer path.

claimAdjudication.claim: All planned controls can pass while corpus-borne instructions influence prose because the envelope is metadata and the leak gate tests copied values/spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Prompt injection, untrusted instructions, imperative rejection, data-only parsing, sanitization, and behavioral non-influence tests.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: De-literalization and fixed authority may incidentally discard imperatives, but neither requires rejection and literal/span signals miss non-literal influence.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A closed data-only schema, instruction rejection, and adversarial behavioral non-influence fixtures cover prompt and draft consumers.

findingDetails.title: Canonical parent surfaces omit the implementation-phase dependency graph
findingDetails.evidence: Child docs declare blocking prerequisites, but the parent handoff and every planned child manual.depends_on array omit them; graph indexing maps only depends_on entries into blocks links.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parent, all phase-004-010 graph/dependency surfaces, child blockers, and the graph blocks consumer were replayed.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical parent and graph metadata cannot represent or surface the implementation ordering required by child packets.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Child dependency tables/blocked tasks, parent transitions, every planned-child graph field, and graph consumer semantics.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child blockers guide humans and limit immediate risk, but graph consumers map only populated depends_on fields into blocks links.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Child-local blockers are proven the sole validated ordering authority and graph blocks are explicitly non-orchestrational/non-retrieval.

findingDetails.title: Two implementation packets classify existing mode and transport surfaces as new creations
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 009 spec/plan and phase 010 spec label existing directory owners Create, while ADR/rollback prose says additions and phase 010 plan says Add.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Action-label search, additive ADR/rollback replay, and current owner reads covered all three surfaces.

claimAdjudication.claim: Owning specs classify three existing runtime surfaces as creations even though planned work is additive, leaving implementation routing contradictory.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Affected-surface matrices, ADR implementation text, rollback semantics, additive requirements, and current owner contracts.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: ADRs and rollback plans strongly contain replacement risk, but owning action labels remain contradictory and additive file boundaries are unnamed.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Dispatch ignores change labels and an authoritative affected-file manifest proves additive-only edits preserving current contracts.

results.summary: Five active P1s survived compact skeptic/referee replay unchanged.
Shared hydration producer lacks root-containment acceptance and negative fixtures.
Literal/span fixtures do not test instruction non-influence.
Resume precedence contains stale continuity; empty dependency metadata suppresses graph blocks links.
Additive intent is clear but owning Create/Add labels conflict.
